title: Create Facial native feature registry and run backbone
operator_request: MT-019 proved the first native Facial bridge but did not implement full Facial parity. Capture a real Handshake-native Facial backbone before adding more feature-specific behavior.

lifecycle.validation_summary: Native Facial registry/run backbone is implemented through product commit 97646510 on feat/WP-CKC-posekit-overhaul. Proof used E:/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target/wtc-ckc-posekit-overhaul because D: artifact root was full. cargo check --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --lib -j 2 passed. cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml facial_native --lib -j 2 -- --nocapture passed 6/6. cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml facial_ingest --lib -j 2 -- --nocapture passed 6/6. git diff --check passed for touched files. Final source-level adversarial re-review passed with Dirac the 2nd and Euler the 2nd.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Read Facial source registry and app spec before implementation. Build native Handshake modules and receipts; do not shell out to facial.exe or Python as the default runtime path.

scope.summary: Replace the broad MT-019 proxy bridge with a native Facial feature registry, feature dispatcher, run state, and artifact/receipt contract that later Facial parity MTs can reuse.
base_scope: Create a Handshake-native Facial feature registry for source families used by the Facial app: facet, python-ofiq, deepface, imagededup, eDifFIQA, YuNet, ArcFace, and PIPNet where applicable.
Add native run/request structures with actor ID, batch ID, profile, selected feature IDs, deterministic item ordering, run status, and artifact refs.
Preserve MT-019 route compatibility while routing through the new registry/backbone where practical.
Represent unsupported or unconfigured models as explicit degraded-state records, not fake results.
Persist run summaries and receipts through Handshake ArtifactStore with source family, feature ID, status, artifact refs, and unsupported reasons.
Keep the backend safe for parallel agents by making run IDs and receipts actor-attributable and deterministic where inputs match.
acceptance_criteria: Feature IDs and source-family names are traceable to Facial source registry/spec.
No Python runtime, external process shellout, or hardcoded machine-local model path is required for the default route.
MT-019 analyze route still works and emits the same or versioned-compatible artifact refs.
Receipts distinguish implemented, unavailable, unsupported, and degraded feature states.
Run records are safe for parallel agents: actor-attributed, deterministic for equal inputs, and recoverable from artifacts.
risks_and_controls: Risk: registry becomes paperwork with no execution path. Control: require route-level test proving MT-019 analysis goes through the registry/run backbone.
Risk: unsupported model features look successful. Control: assert unavailable features produce explicit degraded status and reason fields.
Risk: hardcoded model paths break portability. Control: model paths must be config/env-based and absent by default.
verification: Compare feature IDs against Facial source_app_registry.yaml and app-spec.md.
Backend tests for registry lookup, run creation, degraded unsupported feature records, and MT-019 compatibility.
Artifact/receipt schema snapshot or direct JSON assertion for run status and source-family fields.

manual_requirement: Required if this MT exposes or changes operator/model-visible Facial behavior.

proof: final source-level adversarial re-review PASS from Dirac the 2nd and Euler the 2nd
handoff.notes: src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/atelier.rs was added to this MT path set as a necessary scope expansion for native_run artifact recoverability.
